title: Comcast (CMCSA) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Comcast using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what Comcast is really worth. Economic Prospect Score: 52/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-i7wstfjt>
Comcast faces cord-cutting headwinds, but its broadband and wireless segments provide a stable floor. Theme parks and studios provide cyclical upside. A 3% growth rate reflects a mature business steadily returning capital to shareholders, despite structural challenges in legacy segments.

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a P/E or EV/EBITDA multiple?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Comcast (CMCSA) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.

prospectVerdictDetail: Comcast is a broadband and media conglomerate facing secular headwinds in linear TV (NBCUniversal, cable) offset by broadband stability and Peacock streaming growth. The broadband business has strong margins but faces fiber and fixed wireless competition. Theme parks add diversification.
prospectKeyRisks: Broadband subscriber losses to fiber (AT&T, Google Fiber) and fixed wireless (T-Mobile, Verizon) accelerating
Linear TV advertising and affiliate fee revenue declining as cord-cutting continues
Peacock streaming losses persisting as profitability requires scale that's hard to achieve against Netflix/Disney+
prospectKeyCatalysts: Broadband ARPU growth through speed tier upgrades and converged connectivity bundles offsetting subscriber losses
Peacock reaching profitability as content costs stabilize and advertising tier scales
Theme parks (Universal Epic Universe) driving revenue growth and diversification from media
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
